notes: NEISS 2002-2006 weighted national estimate; rate 5.21 per 100k person-years (95% CI 4.74-5.68); 44.5% sports-related in patients >=10y; second most common adult dislocation; no ISS/analog dislocation data found—terrestrial proxy with upward selection bias from high-energy mechanisms in adolescent athletes

IMM-class simulation SMM Mars ref mission (923d total/401d EVA); incidence 0.00005 per person-day; no observed in-flight elbow dislocations in Scheuring 2009 cohort (219 total musculoskeletal injuries)—model-predicted not observed; conservative for long-duration exploration with EVA

NEISS 2004-2008 explicit denominator 1.499B person-years; rate 11.11 per 100k py (95% CI 9.55-12.6); 78.7% male; 35.9% sports-related (basketball 25.6% football 21.5%); ages 15-19 highest risk (38.6/100k); Taiwan comparison 4.6/100k; no spaceflight analog data—general-pop proxy with known age/sex/activity selection bias

IMM-class simulation SMM Mars ref mission; incidence 0.00008 per person-day; hand injuries most common in-flight musculoskeletal injury (>70 of 219 in Scheuring 2009) but dislocations not enumerated—model treats as terrestrial-fixed extrapolated; accept for tierC

NEISS 2002-2006 raw sample count used as observational unit; incidence 23.9 per 100k py (95% CI 20.8-27.0); 71.8% male; peak 20-29y (47.8/100k); 58.8% from falls; most common joint dislocation; shoulder injuries in astronauts well-documented (>20 in-flight in Scheuring 2009; 9.67 per 100 astronauts over 10y training-related) but dislocation-specific incidence unpublished—proxy carries moderate upward bias from sports/fall mechanisms

IMM-class simulation SMM Mars ref mission (923d/401d EVA); incidence 0.00014 per person-day; EVA training-associated shoulder injuries common (64% of 22 astronauts reported shoulder pain attributed to EVA training; 9.67 per 100 astronauts over 10y) but dislocations not isolated in published cohorts—simulated value likely conservative for exploration
NASA LSAH hip fx. LD >90 days. 2 hip fx in 377 py (42 astronauts). RR 3.4 vs non-LD. Direct spaceflight analog. Very small event count; wide CI.
NASA LSAH hip fx. Non-LD <90 days cumulative. 5 hip fx in 5626 py (232 astronauts). Baseline astronaut hip fx rate.
NASA LSAH hip fx. No-spaceflight period. Zero observed hip fx in 1675 py (262 astronauts). Censored count; true rate near zero given young healthy cohort.

Back-calculated from US population-based hip fracture incidence for men age 45-55 (~60 per 100000 py; Amin 2014 / Farr 2017 cited in NASA HSRB). Astronauts are healthier than general population (healthy worker effect); this likely overestimates risk. Not a direct analog. TierC fallback.

NASA LSAH spine (thoracic+lumbar) fx. LD >90 days. 3 spine fx in 377 py. RR 3.4 for LD vs non-LD. Direct spaceflight analog. Includes thoracic; no pure lumbar count available. Very small event count; wide CI.
NASA LSAH spine (thoracic+lumbar) fx. Non-LD <90 days. 17 spine fx in 5626 py. Baseline astronaut spine fx rate. Includes thoracic.
NASA LSAH spine (thoracic+lumbar) fx. No-spaceflight period. 6 spine fx in 1675 py. Includes thoracic.

Back-calculated from UK 10-year vertebral fracture probability for men age 50 (0.2% over 10 y ~ 0.02%/yr = 20 per 100000 py). Clinical vertebral fx in men 45-55 are rare and heavily under-diagnosed. Astronaut healthy-worker effect means true analog risk likely lower. TierC fallback.

US Army Basic Combat Training male recruits 1997-2007. 19.3 cases per 1000 recruits in 10 weeks. 475745 men. High-loading analog relevant to planetary EVA surface operations. Selection bias: recruits are young and screened for health; still higher rates than expected in analog astronauts.
US Army Basic Combat Training female recruits 1997-2007. 79.9 cases per 1000 recruits in 10 weeks. 107906 women. Applicable as high-loading analog. Selection bias as above. Gender disparity notable.

Female Marine Corps recruits 1999. 66 confirmed LE stress fractures in 824 women over 12-week basic training. 1.0 per 1000 training-day exposures. Smaller cohort but prospective design with training-day exposure denominator. High-loading analog.

Female Navy recruits. RCT of calcium/vitamin D vs placebo. Per-protocol: 285 stress fractures in 3700 women over 8 weeks (6.8% supplemented vs 8.6% placebo). ITT incidence 5.9%. High-loading analog with nutritional intervention context.

All US active-duty service members 2009-2012. 31758 LE stress fractures in 5.58M person-years (5.69 per 1000 py). Includes recruits and qualified personnel. Junior enlisted at highest risk (18.54 per 1000 py Army/Marines). Most applicable as sustained occupational loading analog.

No traumatic abdominal injuries observed in 26.43 person-years of US spaceflight (STS+ISS). 6 minor trunk injuries used as proxy impact rate for IMM biomechanical model. Model estimates mean 2.6e-4 per person-year. Highly selected astronaut population; microgravity reduces fall risk but increases equipment-impact risk. TierA-NASA direct observation.

Norway National Trauma Registry 2015-2023 (9 years). 7086 abdominal injuries in ~5.4M population. Age-sex-adjusted incidence 14.9 per 100000 person-years; solid organ 7.2; hollow viscus 1.2. Includes blunt and penetrating trauma. General population proxy; astronauts are younger and healthier so true rate likely lower.

Germany 2015-2022. 13305 traumatic compartment syndrome cases in 83.05M population over 8 years. Rate 2 per 100000 person-years. National inpatient data (oste synthesis procedures). No spaceflight or analog ACS data found; general trauma population proxy. TierB-lit.

GBD 2019 age-standardized incidence 162.96 per 100000 for inguinal/femoral/abdominal hernia combined. Back-calculated from published rate over 100000 person-years. No spaceflight analog data found. General population proxy; astronauts are younger and selected for health so true rate likely substantially lower.
Aviation hypobaric chamber 502 subjects 20h exposure; LLSQ-defined AMS
Antarctic South Pole 2835m rapid ascent 246 subjects 1st week at altitude 52pct AMS incidence
46 long-duration ISS missions 20.57 flight-years zero AMS events
Olmsted County MN 1983-1987 ~100k population 5-year cohort
Rochester MN population-based cohort incidence 49.8 per 100k person-years
46 long-duration ISS missions 20.57 flight-years zero ARS events
Zero choking events across all NASA/Russian/ISS medical logs (~3000 person-years of spaceflight). Extreme rarity in screened healthy crews eating pre-packaged food. Tier-C proxy.

Population-based FBAO-related OHCA in Denmark 2016-2022 (0.78 per 100k person-years). Back-calculated from rate. Only OHCA cases; true choking incidence including nonfatal is higher. General-pop proxy for rarity.
Prehospital FBAO in San Diego County adults over 17 months. County pop ~2.95M. EMS-attended cases only; mild choking resolved without EMS not captured. 3.3% mortality. General-pop proxy.
Fatal food asphyxiation in Dade County FL over 20 years. Incidence 0.66 per 100k. Fatalities only; institutionalized patients overrepresented. Tier-C proxy.
US adult fatal choking rates: 0.1/100k (18-64y) and 0.7/100k (>65y). Back-calculated weighted average for adult population. Fatalities only. Tier-C general-pop proxy.
No spinal cord injuries in human spaceflight history; therefore zero neurogenic shock. Extreme rarity in analog environment. Tier-C proxy only.

Back-calculated from US SCI incidence 54/million/year (1993-2012) and NS proportion ~16% among all SCI (Guly 2008 cervical 19.3% thoracic/lumbar 7%). Only occurs after SCI; healthy crew incidence is orders of magnitude lower. Tier-C proxy.

TARN trauma registry England & Wales 1989-2003. 490 isolated SCI. NS incidence back-calculated from cervical 19.3% and thoracic/lumbar 7% with estimated 50/50 split. TARN partial coverage; catchment estimated at ~50% of population. Tier-C trauma-registry proxy.
Meta-analysis acute pancreatitis incidence North America 33.74 per 100000 person-years. General-population proxy; no analog data found.
All-types prostatitis incidence 4.9 per 1000 person-years from US claims data. Broader than acute prostatitis alone.
Acute prostatitis and NOS incidence 3.2-3.6 per 1000 person-years. European population. Closer phenotype match than all-types rate.
Septic arthritis incidence in adults ~4 per 100000 person-years. Used as closest proxy for acute arthritis; no direct acute arthritis incidence found.
